#!/bin/bash
cd "$(dirname "$0")"

echo "=== Sentinel AI Suite ==="
echo

# Start local AI stack (thinking_stripper → LiteLLM → Ollama)
pgrep -f "thinking_stripper" > /dev/null || nohup python3 -m uvicorn thinking_stripper:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 4000 --app-dir ~/.claude > /tmp/thinking_stripper.log 2>&1 &
pgrep -f "litellm.*4001"     > /dev/null || nohup litellm --config ~/.claude/litellm_config.yaml --port 4001 --host 0.0.0.0 > /tmp/litellm.log 2>&1 &
export ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN=ollama ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL=http://localhost:4000
export PATH="$HOME/sentinel_files:$PATH"   # exposes `claw` binary
echo "Local AI stack: thinking_stripper → LiteLLM → Ollama"
echo

# Launch sentinel suite
exec bash sentinel_files/start.sh "$@"

#!/bin/bash
cd "$(dirname "$0")"

echo "=== Sentinel AI Suite ==="
echo

# Start local AI stack (thinking_stripper → LiteLLM → Ollama)
pgrep -f "thinking_stripper" > /dev/null || nohup python3 -m uvicorn thinking_stripper:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 4000 --app-dir ~/.claude > /tmp/thinking_stripper.log 2>&1 &
pgrep -f "litellm.*4001"     > /dev/null || nohup litellm --config ~/.claude/litellm_config.yaml --port 4001 --host 0.0.0.0 > /tmp/litellm.log 2>&1 &
export ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN=ollama ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL=http://localhost:4000
export PATH="$HOME/sentinel_files:$PATH"   # exposes `claw` binary
echo "Local AI stack: thinking_stripper → LiteLLM → Ollama"
echo

# Launch sentinel suite
exec bash sentinel_files/start.sh "$@"

content.content:
#!/bin/bash
# ============================================================
#  UNIFIED AI ENVIRONMENT — Master Launcher
#  Starts all services, opens MiroFish in Windows browser.
#
#  Usage:  bash start.sh [--no-browser] [--coding-only]
# ============================================================

set -euo pipefail

BASE="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
LOGS="$BASE/.logs"
mkdir -p "$LOGS"

# ── Colours ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
G='\033[0;32m'; C='\033[0;36m'; Y='\033[1;33m'
R='\033[0;31m'; B='\033[1m'; X='\033[0m'

# ── Flags ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
NO_BROWSER=false
CODING_ONLY=false
for arg in "$@"; do
  case $arg in
    --no-browser)   NO_BROWSER=true ;;
    --coding-only)  CODING_ONLY=true ;;
  esac
done

# ── Helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
log()  { echo -e "${C}[$(date +%H:%M:%S)]${X} $*"; }
ok()   { echo -e "${G}✔${X}  $*"; }
warn() { echo -e "${Y}⚠${X}  $*"; }
err()  { echo -e "${R}✘${X}  $*"; }

wait_port() {
  local port=$1 name=$2 timeout=${3:-30}
  log "Waiting for $name on :$port..."
  for i in $(seq 1 $timeout); do
    if curl -sf "http://localhost:$port" >/dev/null 2>&1 \
        || curl -sf "http://localhost:$port/health" >/dev/null 2>&1 \
        || nc -z localhost "$port" 2>/dev/null; then
      ok "$name is up"
      return 0
    fi
    sleep 1
  done
  warn "$name didn't respond after ${timeout}s (may still be starting)"
  return 1
}

open_browser() {
  local url=$1
  log "Opening $url in Windows browser..."
  # Works from WSL — opens the Windows default browser
  cmd.exe /c start "$url" 2>/dev/null \
    || powershell.exe -Command "Start-Process '$url'" 2>/dev/null \
    || warn "Could not auto-open browser. Visit $url manually."
}

cleanup() {
  echo ""
  warn "Shutting down services..."
  [[ -f "$LOGS/pids.txt" ]] && while read -r pid; do
    kill "$pid" 2>/dev/null && log "Killed PID $pid"
  done < "$LOGS/pids.txt"
  rm -f "$LOGS/pids.txt"
  echo -e "${G}All services stopped.${X}"
}
trap cleanup EXIT INT TERM

# ── Source unified .env ──────────────────────────────────────
if [[ -f "$BASE/.env" ]]; then
  set -o allexport
  source "$BASE/.env"
  set +o allexport
fi

# ── Banner ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
echo ""
echo -e "${B}${C}╔══════════════════════════════════════════════╗${X}"
echo -e "${B}${C}║     UNIFIED AI ENVIRONMENT  — Starting       ║${X}"
echo -e "${B}${C}╚══════════════════════════════════════════════╝${X}"
echo ""

# ── 1. Check Ollama ──────────────────────────────────────────
if curl -sf http://localhost:11434/api/tags >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  ok "Ollama running (deepseek-coder:6.7b available)"
else
  warn "Ollama not running — starting..."
  nohup ollama serve > "$LOGS/ollama.log" 2>&1 &
  echo $! >> "$LOGS/pids.txt"
  wait_port 11434 "Ollama" 20 || true
fi

# ── 2. nanochat_deepseek API (port 8081) ─────────────────────
CODING_DIR="$BASE/nanochat_deepseek"
log "Starting Coding Assistant API (port 8081)..."

# Ensure venv packages are installed
if ! "$CODING_DIR/venv/bin/python" -c "import fastapi" 2>/dev/null; then
  log "Installing coding assistant deps..."
  "$CODING_DIR/venv/bin/pip" install -q \
    fastapi uvicorn pydantic ollama rich chromadb 2>/dev/null || true
fi

nohup "$CODING_DIR/venv/bin/python" "$CODING_DIR/api_server.py" \
  > "$LOGS/coding_assistant.log" 2>&1 &
CODING_PID=$!
echo $CODING_PID >> "$LOGS/pids.txt"
log "Coding Assistant PID: $CODING_PID"

if $CODING_ONLY; then
  wait_port 8081 "Coding Assistant" 15 || true
  echo ""
  echo -e "${B}Coding Assistant API:${X} ${G}http://localhost:8081${X}"
  echo -e "  ${C}POST /v1/chat/completions${X}  (OpenAI-compatible)"
  echo -e "  ${C}GET  /health${X}"
  echo ""
  wait
  exit 0
fi

# ── 3. MiroFish Backend (port 5001, Flask) ───────────────────
MIRO_DIR="$BASE/MiroFish"
log "Starting MiroFish Backend (port 5001)..."

# Detect venv
if   [[ -d "$MIRO_DIR/backend/.venv" ]]; then MIRO_VENV="$MIRO_DIR/backend/.venv"
elif [[ -d "$MIRO_DIR/backend/venv"  ]]; then MIRO_VENV="$MIRO_DIR/backend/venv"
elif [[ -d "$MIRO_DIR/.venv"         ]]; then MIRO_VENV="$MIRO_DIR/.venv"
elif [[ -d "$MIRO_DIR/venv"          ]]; then MIRO_VENV="$MIRO_DIR/venv"
else
  warn "No venv found for MiroFish — installing via uv..."
  cd "$MIRO_DIR" && uv sync --quiet 2>/dev/null || true
  MIRO_VENV="$MIRO_DIR/backend/venv"
fi

cd "$MIRO_DIR"
nohup "$MIRO_VENV/bin/python" "$MIRO_DIR/backend/run.py" \
  > "$LOGS/mirofish_backend.log" 2>&1 &
MIRO_BACKEND_PID=$!
echo $MIRO_BACKEND_PID >> "$LOGS/pids.txt"
log "MiroFish Backend PID: $MIRO_BACKEND_PID"

# ── 4. Wait for backend ──────────────────────────────────────
wait_port 5001 "MiroFish Backend" 30 || true

# ── 5. MiroFish Frontend (port 3000, Vite/React) ─────────────
log "Starting MiroFish Frontend (port 3000)..."
FRONTEND_DIR="$MIRO_DIR/frontend"

# Install node_modules if missing
if [[ ! -d "$FRONTEND_DIR/node_modules" ]]; then
  log "Installing frontend dependencies..."
  cd "$FRONTEND_DIR" && npm install --silent
fi

cd "$FRONTEND_DIR"
nohup npm run dev -- --port 3000 \
  > "$LOGS/mirofish_frontend.log" 2>&1 &
MIRO_FRONTEND_PID=$!
echo $MIRO_FRONTEND_PID >> "$LOGS/pids.txt"
log "MiroFish Frontend PID: $MIRO_FRONTEND_PID"

# ── 6. Wait then open browser ────────────────────────────────
wait_port 3000 "MiroFish Frontend" 45

if ! $NO_BROWSER; then
  sleep 1
  open_browser "http://localhost:3000"
fi

# ── 7. Sentinel Agent (claude --print wrapper, port 8080) ────
AGENT_DIR="$BASE/claude_agent"
log "Starting Sentinel Agent (port 8080)..."

if [[ ! -d "$AGENT_DIR/venv" ]]; then
  log "Creating Sentinel Agent venv..."
  python3 -m venv "$AGENT_DIR/venv"
  "$AGENT_DIR/venv/bin/pip" install -q -r "$AGENT_DIR/requirements.txt"
fi

nohup "$AGENT_DIR/venv/bin/python" "$AGENT_DIR/server.py" \
  > "$LOGS/sentinel_agent.log" 2>&1 &
AGENT_PID=$!
echo $AGENT_PID >> "$LOGS/pids.txt"
wait_port 8080 "Sentinel Agent" 15 || true

# ── 8. Dashboard ─────────────────────────────────────────────
echo ""
echo -e "${B}${G}╔══════════════════════════════════════════════╗${X}"
echo -e "${B}${G}║        ALL SERVICES RUNNING                  ║${X}"
echo -e "${B}${G}╚══════════════════════════════════════════════╝${X}"
echo ""
echo -e "  ${G}●${X} ${B}MiroFish${X}            http://localhost:3000  (main UI)"
echo -e "  ${G}●${X} ${B}MiroFish API${X}        http://localhost:5001"
echo -e "  ${G}●${X} ${B}Coding Assistant${X}    http://localhost:8081  (OpenAI-compatible)"

toolUseResult.stdout:
#!/bin/bash
# ============================================================
#  UNIFIED AI ENVIRONMENT — Master Launcher
#  Starts all services, opens MiroFish in Windows browser.
#
#  Usage:  bash start.sh [--no-browser] [--coding-only]
# ============================================================

set -euo pipefail

BASE="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
LOGS="$BASE/.logs"
mkdir -p "$LOGS"

# ── Colours ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
G='\033[0;32m'; C='\033[0;36m'; Y='\033[1;33m'
R='\033[0;31m'; B='\033[1m'; X='\033[0m'

# ── Flags ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
NO_BROWSER=false
CODING_ONLY=false
for arg in "$@"; do
  case $arg in
    --no-browser)   NO_BROWSER=true ;;
    --coding-only)  CODING_ONLY=true ;;
  esac
done

# ── Helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
log()  { echo -e "${C}[$(date +%H:%M:%S)]${X} $*"; }
ok()   { echo -e "${G}✔${X}  $*"; }
warn() { echo -e "${Y}⚠${X}  $*"; }
err()  { echo -e "${R}✘${X}  $*"; }

wait_port() {
  local port=$1 name=$2 timeout=${3:-30}
  log "Waiting for $name on :$port..."
  for i in $(seq 1 $timeout); do
    if curl -sf "http://localhost:$port" >/dev/null 2>&1 \
        || curl -sf "http://localhost:$port/health" >/dev/null 2>&1 \
        || nc -z localhost "$port" 2>/dev/null; then
      ok "$name is up"
      return 0
    fi
    sleep 1
  done
  warn "$name didn't respond after ${timeout}s (may still be starting)"
  return 1
}

open_browser() {
  local url=$1
  log "Opening $url in Windows browser..."
  # Works from WSL — opens the Windows default browser
  cmd.exe /c start "$url" 2>/dev/null \
    || powershell.exe -Command "Start-Process '$url'" 2>/dev/null \
    || warn "Could not auto-open browser. Visit $url manually."
}

cleanup() {
  echo ""
  warn "Shutting down services..."
  [[ -f "$LOGS/pids.txt" ]] && while read -r pid; do
    kill "$pid" 2>/dev/null && log "Killed PID $pid"
  done < "$LOGS/pids.txt"
  rm -f "$LOGS/pids.txt"
  echo -e "${G}All services stopped.${X}"
}
trap cleanup EXIT INT TERM

# ── Source unified .env ──────────────────────────────────────
if [[ -f "$BASE/.env" ]]; then
  set -o allexport
  source "$BASE/.env"
  set +o allexport
fi

# ── Banner ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
echo ""
echo -e "${B}${C}╔══════════════════════════════════════════════╗${X}"
echo -e "${B}${C}║     UNIFIED AI ENVIRONMENT  — Starting       ║${X}"
echo -e "${B}${C}╚══════════════════════════════════════════════╝${X}"
echo ""

# ── 1. Check Ollama ──────────────────────────────────────────
if curl -sf http://localhost:11434/api/tags >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  ok "Ollama running (deepseek-coder:6.7b available)"
else
  warn "Ollama not running — starting..."
  nohup ollama serve > "$LOGS/ollama.log" 2>&1 &
  echo $! >> "$LOGS/pids.txt"
  wait_port 11434 "Ollama" 20 || true
fi

# ── 2. nanochat_deepseek API (port 8081) ─────────────────────
CODING_DIR="$BASE/nanochat_deepseek"
log "Starting Coding Assistant API (port 8081)..."

# Ensure venv packages are installed
if ! "$CODING_DIR/venv/bin/python" -c "import fastapi" 2>/dev/null; then
  log "Installing coding assistant deps..."
  "$CODING_DIR/venv/bin/pip" install -q \
    fastapi uvicorn pydantic ollama rich chromadb 2>/dev/null || true
fi

nohup "$CODING_DIR/venv/bin/python" "$CODING_DIR/api_server.py" \
  > "$LOGS/coding_assistant.log" 2>&1 &
CODING_PID=$!
echo $CODING_PID >> "$LOGS/pids.txt"
log "Coding Assistant PID: $CODING_PID"

if $CODING_ONLY; then
  wait_port 8081 "Coding Assistant" 15 || true
  echo ""
  echo -e "${B}Coding Assistant API:${X} ${G}http://localhost:8081${X}"
  echo -e "  ${C}POST /v1/chat/completions${X}  (OpenAI-compatible)"
  echo -e "  ${C}GET  /health${X}"
  echo ""
  wait
  exit 0
fi

# ── 3. MiroFish Backend (port 5001, Flask) ───────────────────
MIRO_DIR="$BASE/MiroFish"
log "Starting MiroFish Backend (port 5001)..."

# Detect venv
if   [[ -d "$MIRO_DIR/backend/.venv" ]]; then MIRO_VENV="$MIRO_DIR/backend/.venv"
elif [[ -d "$MIRO_DIR/backend/venv"  ]]; then MIRO_VENV="$MIRO_DIR/backend/venv"
elif [[ -d "$MIRO_DIR/.venv"         ]]; then MIRO_VENV="$MIRO_DIR/.venv"
elif [[ -d "$MIRO_DIR/venv"          ]]; then MIRO_VENV="$MIRO_DIR/venv"
else
  warn "No venv found for MiroFish — installing via uv..."
  cd "$MIRO_DIR" && uv sync --quiet 2>/dev/null || true
  MIRO_VENV="$MIRO_DIR/backend/venv"
fi

cd "$MIRO_DIR"
nohup "$MIRO_VENV/bin/python" "$MIRO_DIR/backend/run.py" \
  > "$LOGS/mirofish_backend.log" 2>&1 &
MIRO_BACKEND_PID=$!
echo $MIRO_BACKEND_PID >> "$LOGS/pids.txt"
log "MiroFish Backend PID: $MIRO_BACKEND_PID"

# ── 4. Wait for backend ──────────────────────────────────────
wait_port 5001 "MiroFish Backend" 30 || true

# ── 5. MiroFish Frontend (port 3000, Vite/React) ─────────────
log "Starting MiroFish Frontend (port 3000)..."
FRONTEND_DIR="$MIRO_DIR/frontend"

# Install node_modules if missing
if [[ ! -d "$FRONTEND_DIR/node_modules" ]]; then
  log "Installing frontend dependencies..."
  cd "$FRONTEND_DIR" && npm install --silent
fi

cd "$FRONTEND_DIR"
nohup npm run dev -- --port 3000 \
  > "$LOGS/mirofish_frontend.log" 2>&1 &
MIRO_FRONTEND_PID=$!
echo $MIRO_FRONTEND_PID >> "$LOGS/pids.txt"
log "MiroFish Frontend PID: $MIRO_FRONTEND_PID"

# ── 6. Wait then open browser ────────────────────────────────
wait_port 3000 "MiroFish Frontend" 45

if ! $NO_BROWSER; then
  sleep 1
  open_browser "http://localhost:3000"
fi

# ── 7. Sentinel Agent (claude --print wrapper, port 8080) ────
AGENT_DIR="$BASE/claude_agent"
log "Starting Sentinel Agent (port 8080)..."

if [[ ! -d "$AGENT_DIR/venv" ]]; then
  log "Creating Sentinel Agent venv..."
  python3 -m venv "$AGENT_DIR/venv"
  "$AGENT_DIR/venv/bin/pip" install -q -r "$AGENT_DIR/requirements.txt"
fi

nohup "$AGENT_DIR/venv/bin/python" "$AGENT_DIR/server.py" \
  > "$LOGS/sentinel_agent.log" 2>&1 &
AGENT_PID=$!
echo $AGENT_PID >> "$LOGS/pids.txt"
wait_port 8080 "Sentinel Agent" 15 || true

# ── 8. Dashboard ─────────────────────────────────────────────
echo ""
echo -e "${B}${G}╔══════════════════════════════════════════════╗${X}"
echo -e "${B}${G}║        ALL SERVICES RUNNING                  ║${X}"
echo -e "${B}${G}╚══════════════════════════════════════════════╝${X}"
echo ""
echo -e "  ${G}●${X} ${B}MiroFish${X}            http://localhost:3000  (main UI)"
echo -e "  ${G}●${X} ${B}MiroFish API${X}        http://localhost:5001"
echo -e "  ${G}●${X} ${B}Coding Assistant${X}    http://localhost:8081  (OpenAI-compatible)"
echo -e "  ${G}●${X} ${B}Sentinel Agent${X}      http://localhost:8080  (claude → local LLM)"
echo -e "  ${G}●${X} ${B}Ollama${X}              http://localhost:11434"
echo ""
echo -e "  ${Y}Logs:${X} $LOGS/"
echo -e "  ${C}Run tests:${X} bash $BASE/test.sh"
echo ""
echo -e "  ${R}Press Ctrl+C to stop all services${X}"
echo ""

# Keep running so trap fires on Ctrl+C
wait

requirements.txt
server.py
venv
---
"""
Sentinel Agent Server — port 8080
Wraps `claude --print` as an OpenAI-compatible HTTP API.
Request path: client → here (8080) → claude --print → thinking_stripper (4000) → LiteLLM (4001) → Ollama
"""

import asyncio
import json
import os
import re
import time
import uuid

import uvicorn
from fastapi import FastAPI, HTTPException, Request
from fastapi.responses import StreamingResponse
from pydantic import BaseModel

_ANSI_RE = re.compile(r"(\x9B|\x1B\[)[0-?]*[ -/]*[@-~]|[\x07-\x0d]|[\x1b][@-_]")

app = FastAPI(title="Sentinel Agent", version="1.0.0")

SENTINEL_DIR = os.path.dirname(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__)))

CLAUDE_ENV = {
    **os.environ,
    "ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN": "ollama",
    "ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL": "http://localhost:4000",
    "PATH": f"{SENTINEL_DIR}:{os.environ.get('PATH', '')}",  # exposes claw
}

# Prefer claw if available, fall back to claude
def _resolve_backend() -> tuple[str, str]:
    """Returns (binary, name) for whichever AI CLI is available."""
    import shutil
    claw = os.path.join(SENTINEL_DIR, "claw")
    if os.path.isfile(claw) and os.access(claw, os.X_OK):
        return claw, "claw"
    if shutil.which("claude"):
        return "claude", "claude"
    raise RuntimeError("Neither claw nor claude CLI found")


# ── Request models ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

class ChatMessage(BaseModel):
    role: str
    content: str


class ChatRequest(BaseModel):
    model: str = "claude-sonnet-4-6"
    messages: list[ChatMessage]
    stream: bool = False
    max_tokens: int | None = None


class PromptRequest(BaseModel):
    prompt: str
    cwd: str | None = None

content.text:
Here's the full picture:

**What works:**
- Ollama (port 11434) — running with `qwen2.5:7b`, `qwen2.5-coder:14b`, `deepseek-r1:14b`
- thinking_stripper (port 4000) — running, inference passes through
- LiteLLM (port 4001) — running, inference works end-to-end
- `claw` binary — works correctly with local AI backend
- Sentinel Agent (port 8080) — starts fine, just **not currently running**
- MiroFish backend (port 5001) — starts fine, just **not currently running**
- MiroFish frontend (port 3000) — dependencies installed, ready

**What's broken:**
- `nanochat_deepseek/` directory is **missing** — `start.sh` expects it at `sentinel_files/nanochat_deepseek/` but it doesn't exist. Since `start.sh` uses `set -euo pipefail`, it will crash before starting MiroFish or the Sentinel Agent.
- `start_sentinel.sh` also uses a relative path (`sentinel_files/start.sh`) so it must be run from `/home/nugad/`.

**To launch what works now:**

```bash
cd /home/nugad && bash start_sentinel.sh --coding-only
```
The `--coding-only` flag skips nanochat (port 8081), MiroFish, and just starts the local AI stack + Sentinel Agent.

Or to run everything except nanochat, you need to either:
1. **Comment out the nanochat section** in `sentinel_files/start.sh` (lines starting with `# ── 2. nanochat_deepseek API`), or
2. **Create a stub** `nanochat_deepseek/` directory

Want me to patch `start.sh` to skip the missing `nanochat_deepseek` gracefully so `bash start_sentinel.sh` launches all other services cleanly?

1	#!/bin/bash
2	# ============================================================
3	#  UNIFIED AI ENVIRONMENT — Master Launcher
4	#  Starts all services, opens MiroFish in Windows browser.
5	#
6	#  Usage:  bash start.sh [--no-browser] [--coding-only]
7	# ============================================================
8	
9	set -euo pipefail
10	
11	BASE="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
12	LOGS="$BASE/.logs"
13	mkdir -p "$LOGS"
14	
15	# ── Colours ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
16	G='\033[0;32m'; C='\033[0;36m'; Y='\033[1;33m'
17	R='\033[0;31m'; B='\033[1m'; X='\033[0m'
18	
19	# ── Flags ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
20	NO_BROWSER=false
21	CODING_ONLY=false
22	for arg in "$@"; do
23	  case $arg in
24	    --no-browser)   NO_BROWSER=true ;;
25	    --coding-only)  CODING_ONLY=true ;;
26	  esac
27	done
28	
29	# ── Helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
30	log()  { echo -e "${C}[$(date +%H:%M:%S)]${X} $*"; }
31	ok()   { echo -e "${G}✔${X}  $*"; }
32	warn() { echo -e "${Y}⚠${X}  $*"; }
33	err()  { echo -e "${R}✘${X}  $*"; }
34	
35	wait_port() {
36	  local port=$1 name=$2 timeout=${3:-30}
37	  log "Waiting for $name on :$port..."
38	  for i in $(seq 1 $timeout); do
39	    if curl -sf "http://localhost:$port" >/dev/null 2>&1 \
40	        || curl -sf "http://localhost:$port/health" >/dev/null 2>&1 \
41	        || nc -z localhost "$port" 2>/dev/null; then
42	      ok "$name is up"
43	      return 0
44	    fi
45	    sleep 1
46	  done
47	  warn "$name didn't respond after ${timeout}s (may still be starting)"
48	  return 1
49	}
50	
51	open_browser() {
52	  local url=$1
53	  log "Opening $url in Windows browser..."
54	  # Works from WSL — opens the Windows default browser
55	  cmd.exe /c start "$url" 2>/dev/null \
56	    || powershell.exe -Command "Start-Process '$url'" 2>/dev/null \
57	    || warn "Could not auto-open browser. Visit $url manually."
58	}
59	
60	cleanup() {
61	  echo ""
62	  warn "Shutting down services..."
63	  [[ -f "$LOGS/pids.txt" ]] && while read -r pid; do
64	    kill "$pid" 2>/dev/null && log "Killed PID $pid"
65	  done < "$LOGS/pids.txt"
66	  rm -f "$LOGS/pids.txt"
67	  echo -e "${G}All services stopped.${X}"
68	}
69	trap cleanup EXIT INT TERM
70	
71	# ── Source unified .env ──────────────────────────────────────
72	if [[ -f "$BASE/.env" ]]; then
73	  set -o allexport
74	  source "$BASE/.env"
75	  set +o allexport
76	fi
77	
78	# ── Banner ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
79	echo ""
80	echo -e "${B}${C}╔══════════════════════════════════════════════╗${X}"
81	echo -e "${B}${C}║     UNIFIED AI ENVIRONMENT  — Starting       ║${X}"
82	echo -e "${B}${C}╚══════════════════════════════════════════════╝${X}"
83	echo ""
84	
85	# ── 1. Check Ollama ──────────────────────────────────────────
86	if curl -sf http://localhost:11434/api/tags >/dev/null 2>&1; then
87	  ok "Ollama running (deepseek-coder:6.7b available)"
88	else

91	  echo $! >> "$LOGS/pids.txt"

93	fi
94	
95	# ── 2. nanochat_deepseek API (port 8081) ─────────────────────
96	CODING_DIR="$BASE/nanochat_deepseek"
97	log "Starting Coding Assistant API (port 8081)..."
98	
99	# Ensure venv packages are installed

101	  log "Installing coding assistant deps..."
102	  "$CODING_DIR/venv/bin/pip" install -q \
103	    fastapi uvicorn pydantic ollama rich chromadb 2>/dev/null || true
104	fi
105	
106	nohup "$CODING_DIR/venv/bin/python" "$CODING_DIR/api_server.py" \
107	  > "$LOGS/coding_assistant.log" 2>&1 &
108	CODING_PID=$!

114	  echo ""

116	  echo -e "  ${C}POST /v1/chat/completions${X}  (OpenAI-compatible)"
117	  echo -e "  ${C}GET  /health${X}"
118	  echo ""

125	log "Starting MiroFish Backend (port 5001)..."

131	elif [[ -d "$MIRO_DIR/venv"          ]]; then MIRO_VENV="$MIRO_DIR/venv"

143	log "MiroFish Backend PID: $MIRO_BACKEND_PID"

163	log "MiroFish Frontend PID: $MIRO_FRONTEND_PID"

message.content: Build a 3D generative AI agent powered by Claude that creates Blender scenes from text prompts or images. The agent should support importing user-provided images and .glb 3D asset files as scene components. It must use an emergent, part-based composition system — meaning complex objects are constructed by generating and assembling atomic parts (e.g. a bicycle emerges from a frame, two tyres, handlebars, a seat, and pedals joined together with spatial constraints). The same emergent logic should apply to organic forms like a female human body, built from parts such as the pelvis, ribcage, abdomen, limbs, skull, and surface skin — assembled using metaballs for seamless organic blending, with correct female proportions (wider hips than shoulders, hourglass waist, fuller thighs). The agent should output a structured scene graph JSON describing each part's shape, dimensions, position, material, and attachment relationships, then compile that into an executable Blender Python (bpy) script run headlessly via CLI. It should support multi-turn refinement so users can iteratively modify the scene through natural language. The full stack should include a Python or Node.js backend, a FastAPI or Express API layer, a React frontend with GLB drag-and-drop and render preview, and Claude's vision API for image understanding.

SCENE_GRAPH_SCHEMA = """
{
  "scene_id": "<uuid string>",
  "description": "<one-line scene description>",
  "camera": {
    "location": [x, y, z],
    "look_at": [x, y, z],
    "lens_mm": 50
  },
  "lighting": {
    "sun_energy": 3.0,
    "sun_rotation_euler": [0.6, 0, 0.8],
    "ambient_strength": 0.3
  },
  "objects": [
    {
      "id": "<unique snake_case id>",
      "name": "<human readable name>",
      "type": "cylinder | sphere | cube | torus | cone | plane | metaball | imported_glb",
      "params": {
        // cylinder:  { "radius": float, "depth": float }
        // sphere:    { "radius": float }
        // cube:      { "size": [x, y, z] }
        // torus:     { "major_radius": float, "minor_radius": float }
        // cone:      { "radius1": float, "radius2": float, "depth": float }
        // plane:     { "size": float }
        // metaball:  { "radius": float, "stiffness": float }
        // imported_glb: { "filepath": "<upload path>" }
      },
      "metaball_group": "<shared group name for metaballs that blend together, null otherwise>",
      "position": [x, y, z],
      "rotation_euler": [rx, ry, rz],
      "scale": [sx, sy, sz],
      "parent": "<parent object id or null>",
      "material": {
        "name": "<material name>",
        "base_color": [r, g, b, a],
        "roughness": float,
        "metallic": float,
        "emission_color": [r, g, b, a],
        "emission_strength": float,
        "subsurface": float,
        "subsurface_color": [r, g, b, a],
        "alpha": float
      }
    }
  ]
}
"""


def get_template_context() -> str:
    """Return a formatted string of all templates for Claude's system prompt."""
    lines = ["## Known Assembly Templates\n"]
    for key, tpl in PART_TEMPLATES.items():
        lines.append(f"### {key}\n{tpl['description']}\n")
        parts_preview = [p["id"] for p in tpl["parts"]]
        lines.append(f"Parts: {', '.join(parts_preview)}\n")
    return "\n".join(lines)

            {"id": "knee_L",    "type": "metaball", "params": {"radius": 0.075, "stiffness": 0.9},
             "position": [0.115, 0, 0.5], "material_group": "skin"},
            {"id": "knee_R",    "type": "metaball", "params": {"radius": 0.075, "stiffness": 0.9},

            {"id": "calf_L",    "type": "metaball", "params": {"radius": 0.065, "stiffness": 0.85},

        "parts": [

             "position": [0, 0, 0.6],

            {"id": "canopy_lo","type": "sphere",   "params": {"radius": 0.7},
             "position": [0, 0, 1.7],
             "material": {"base_color": [0.1, 0.55, 0.1, 1], "roughness": 0.9, "metallic": 0}},
            {"id": "canopy_mid","type": "sphere",  "params": {"radius": 0.55},

}


SCENE_GRAPH_SCHEMA = """
{
  "scene_id": "<uuid string>",
  "description": "<one-line scene description>",
  "camera": {
    "location": [x, y, z],
    "look_at": [x, y, z],
    "lens_mm": 50
  },
  "lighting": {
    "sun_energy": 3.0,
    "sun_rotation_euler": [0.6, 0, 0.8],
    "ambient_strength": 0.3
  },
  "objects": [
    {
      "id": "<unique snake_case id>",
      "name": "<human readable name>",
      "type": "cylinder | sphere | cube | torus | cone | plane | metaball | imported_glb",
      "params": {
        // cylinder:  { "radius": float, "depth": float }
        // sphere:    { "radius": float }
        // cube:      { "size": [x, y, z] }
        // torus:     { "major_radius": float, "minor_radius": float }
        // cone:      { "radius1": float, "radius2": float, "depth": float }
        // plane:     { "size": float }
        // metaball:  { "radius": float, "stiffness": float }
        // imported_glb: { "filepath": "<upload path>" }
      },
      "metaball_group": "<shared group name for metaballs that blend together, null otherwise>",
      "position": [x, y, z],
      "rotation_euler": [rx, ry, rz],
      "scale": [sx, sy, sz],
      "parent": "<parent object id or null>",
      "material": {
        "name": "<material name>",
        "base_color": [r, g, b, a],
        "roughness": float,
        "metallic": float,
        "emission_color": [r, g, b, a],
        "emission_strength": float,
        "subsurface": float,
        "subsurface_color": [r, g, b, a],
        "alpha": float
      }
    }
  ]
}
"""


def get_template_context() -> str:
    """Return a formatted string of all templates for Claude's system prompt."""
    lines = ["## Known Assembly Templates\n"]
    for key, tpl in PART_TEMPLATES.items():
        lines.append(f"### {key}\n{tpl['description']}\n")
        parts_preview = [p["id"] for p in tpl["parts"]]
        lines.append(f"Parts: {', '.join(parts_preview)}\n")
    return "\n".join(lines)

input.content:
"""
compiler.py — Converts a scene graph JSON dict into an executable Blender Python script.

Supported types: cylinder, sphere, cube, torus, cone, plane, metaball, imported_glb.
Output: a .py file that, when run headlessly via `blender --background --python script.py`,
produces a .glb render output.
"""

from __future__ import annotations
import json
import textwrap
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helpers
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _vec3(v: list | None, default=(0, 0, 0)) -> tuple:
    if v and len(v) >= 3:
        return (float(v[0]), float(v[1]), float(v[2]))
    return default

def _vec4(v: list | None, default=(1, 1, 1, 1)) -> tuple:
    if v and len(v) >= 4:
        return tuple(float(x) for x in v[:4])
    if v and len(v) == 3:
        return (float(v[0]), float(v[1]), float(v[2]), 1.0)
    return default

def _mat_var(obj_id: str) -> str:
    return f"mat_{obj_id}"

def _obj_var(obj_id: str) -> str:
    return f"obj_{obj_id}"


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Material block
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _material_block(obj_id: str, mat: dict) -> str:
    var = _mat_var(obj_id)
    name = mat.get("name", f"mat_{obj_id}")
    bc = _vec4(mat.get("base_color"))
    roughness = float(mat.get("roughness", 0.5))
    metallic = float(mat.get("metallic", 0.0))
    alpha = float(mat.get("alpha", 1.0))
    emission_color = _vec4(mat.get("emission_color", [0, 0, 0, 1]))
    emission_strength = float(mat.get("emission_strength", 0.0))
    subsurface = float(mat.get("subsurface", 0.0))
    ssc = _vec4(mat.get("subsurface_color", [1, 0.8, 0.7, 1]))

    lines = [
        f'{var} = bpy.data.materials.new(name="{name}")',
        f"{var}.use_nodes = True",
        f"_bsdf = {var}.node_tree.nodes['Principled BSDF']",
        f"_bsdf.inputs['Base Color'].default_value = {bc}",
        f"_bsdf.inputs['Roughness'].default_value = {roughness}",
        f"_bsdf.inputs['Metallic'].default_value = {metallic}",
        f"_bsdf.inputs['Alpha'].default_value = {alpha}",
    ]
    if emission_strength > 0:
        lines += [
            f"_bsdf.inputs['Emission Color'].default_value = {emission_color}",
            f"_bsdf.inputs['Emission Strength'].default_value = {emission_strength}",
        ]
    if subsurface > 0:
        lines += [
            f"_bsdf.inputs['Subsurface Weight'].default_value = {subsurface}",
            f"_bsdf.inputs['Subsurface Radius'].default_value = {ssc[:3]}",
        ]
    return "\n".join(lines)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Object creation blocks per type
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _cylinder_block(obj_id: str, obj: dict) -> str:
    params = obj.get("params", {})
    radius = float(params.get("radius", 0.5))
    depth = float(params.get("depth", 1.0))
    loc = _vec3(obj.get("position"))
    var = _obj_var(obj_id)
    return "\n".join([
        f"bpy.ops.mesh.primitive_cylinder_add(radius={radius}, depth={depth}, location={loc})",
        f"{var} = bpy.context.active_object",
        f'{var}.name = "{obj_id}"',
    ])


def _sphere_block(obj_id: str, obj: dict) -> str:
    params = obj.get("params", {})
    radius = float(params.get("radius", 0.5))
    loc = _vec3(obj.get("position"))
    var = _obj_var(obj_id)
    return "\n".join([
        f"bpy.ops.mesh.primitive_uv_sphere_add(radius={radius}, location={loc})",
        f"{var} = bpy.context.active_object",
        f'{var}.name = "{obj_id}"',
    ])


def _cube_block(obj_id: str, obj: dict) -> str:
    params = obj.get("params", {})
    size = params.get("size", [1, 1, 1])
    if isinstance(size, (int, float)):
        size = [size, size, size]
    loc = _vec3(obj.get("position"))
    var = _obj_var(obj_id)
    # Create unit cube then scale to desired size
    sx, sy, sz = float(size[0]), float(size[1]), float(size[2])
    return "\n".join([
        f"bpy.ops.mesh.primitive_cube_add(size=1, location={loc})",
        f"{var} = bpy.context.active_object",
        f'{var}.name = "{obj_id}"',
        f"{var}.scale = ({sx}, {sy}, {sz})",
        f"bpy.ops.object.transform_apply(scale=True)",
    ])


def _torus_block(obj_id: str, obj: dict) -> str:
    params = obj.get("params", {})
    major_r = float(params.get("major_radius", 0.5))
    minor_r = float(params.get("minor_radius", 0.1))
    loc = _vec3(obj.get("position"))
    var = _obj_var(obj_id)
    return "\n".join([
        f"bpy.ops.mesh.primitive_torus_add("
        f"major_radius={major_r}, minor_radius={minor_r}, location={loc})",
        f"{var} = bpy.context.active_object",
        f'{var}.name = "{obj_id}"',
    ])


def _cone_block(obj_id: str, obj: dict) -> str:
    params = obj.get("params", {})
    r1 = float(params.get("radius1", 0.5))
    r2 = float(params.get("radius2", 0.0))
    depth = float(params.get("depth", 1.0))
    loc = _vec3(obj.get("position"))
    var = _obj_var(obj_id)
    return "\n".join([
        f"bpy.ops.mesh.primitive_cone_add("
        f"radius1={r1}, radius2={r2}, depth={depth}, location={loc})",
        f"{var} = bpy.context.active_object",
        f'{var}.name = "{obj_id}"',
    ])


def _plane_block(obj_id: str, obj: dict) -> str:
    params = obj.get("params", {})
    size = float(params.get("size", 2.0))
    loc = _vec3(obj.get("position"))
    var = _obj_var(obj_id)
    return "\n".join([
        f"bpy.ops.mesh.primitive_plane_add(size={size}, location={loc})",
        f"{var} = bpy.context.active_object",
        f'{var}.name = "{obj_id}"',
    ])


def _metaball_block(obj_id: str, obj: dict, group_vars: dict[str, str]) -> str:
    """
    Metaballs in a shared group all blend seamlessly. We reuse one bpy.data.metaballs
    object per group and add elements to it.
    """
    params = obj.get("params", {})
    radius = float(params.get("radius", 0.3))
    stiffness = float(params.get("stiffness", 1.0))
    pos = _vec3(obj.get("position"))
    group = obj.get("metaball_group", obj_id)
    var = _obj_var(obj_id)

    if group not in group_vars:
        # First metaball in this group — create the metaball data object
        mball_data_var = f"mball_{group}"
        mball_obj_var = f"mball_obj_{group}"
        group_vars[group] = (mball_data_var, mball_obj_var)
        lines = [
            f'{mball_data_var} = bpy.data.metaballs.new("{group}")',
            f'{mball_data_var}.resolution = 0.05',
            f'{mball_data_var}.render_resolution = 0.02',
            f'{mball_obj_var} = bpy.data.objects.new("{group}", {mball_data_var})',
            f"bpy.context.collection.objects.link({mball_obj_var})",
        ]
    else:
        mball_data_var, mball_obj_var = group_vars[group]
        lines = []

    elem_var = f"elem_{obj_id}"
    lines += [
        f"{elem_var} = {mball_data_var}.elements.new()",
        f"{elem_var}.type = 'BALL'",
        f"{elem_var}.co = {pos}",
        f"{elem_var}.radius = {radius}",
        f"{elem_var}.stiffness = {stiffness}",
        f"{var} = {mball_obj_var}",   # alias so material assignment works uniformly
    ]
    return "\n".join(lines)


def _imported_glb_block(obj_id: str, obj: dict) -> str:
    params = obj.get("params", {})
    filepath = params.get("filepath", "")
    loc = _vec3(obj.get("position"))
    var = _obj_var(obj_id)
    return "\n".join([
        f'bpy.ops.import_scene.gltf(filepath="{filepath}")',
        f"_imported = bpy.context.selected_objects",
        f"{var} = _imported[0] if _imported else None",
        f"if {var}:",
        f'    {var}.name = "{obj_id}"',
        f"    {var}.location = {loc}",
    ])


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Rotation / scale / parent
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _transform_block(obj_id: str, obj: dict) -> str:
    var = _obj_var(obj_id)
    lines = []
    rot = obj.get("rotation_euler")
    if rot and any(v != 0 for v in rot):
        rx, ry, rz = float(rot[0]), float(rot[1]), float(rot[2])
        lines.append(f"{var}.rotation_euler = ({rx}, {ry}, {rz})")

    scale = obj.get("scale")
    if scale and scale != [1, 1, 1]:
        sx, sy, sz = float(scale[0]), float(scale[1]), float(scale[2])
        lines.append(f"{var}.scale = ({sx}, {sy}, {sz})")
    return "\n".join(lines)


def _parent_block(obj_id: str, parent_id: str | None) -> str:
    if not parent_id:
        return ""
    var = _obj_var(obj_id)
    pvar = _obj_var(parent_id)
    return "\n".join([
        f"if {pvar} and {var}:",
        f"    {var}.parent = {pvar}",
        f"    {var}.matrix_parent_inverse = {pvar}.matrix_world.inverted()",
    ])


def _material_assign_block(obj_id: str) -> str:
    var = _obj_var(obj_id)
    mvar = _mat_var(obj_id)
    return "\n".join([
        f"if {var} and {var}.data and hasattr({var}.data, 'materials'):",
        f"    if {var}.data.materials:",
        f"        {var}.data.materials[0] = {mvar}",
        f"    else:",
        f"        {var}.data.materials.append({mvar})",
    ])


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Camera and lighting
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _camera_block(camera: dict) -> str:
    loc = _vec3(camera.get("location", [3, -5, 3]))
    look_at = _vec3(camera.get("look_at", [0, 0, 0]))
    lens = float(camera.get("lens_mm", 50))
    return textwrap.dedent(f"""
        # Camera
        bpy.ops.object.camera_add(location={loc})
        _cam = bpy.context.active_object
        _cam.name = "SceneCamera"
        _cam.data.lens = {lens}
        import mathutils
        _direction = mathutils.Vector({look_at}) - mathutils.Vector({loc})
        _rot_quat = _direction.to_track_quat('-Z', 'Y')
        _cam.rotation_euler = _rot_quat.to_euler()
        bpy.context.scene.camera = _cam
    """).strip()


def _lighting_block(lighting: dict) -> str:
    energy = float(lighting.get("sun_energy", 3.0))
    rot = _vec3(lighting.get("sun_rotation_euler", [0.6, 0, 0.8]))
    ambient = float(lighting.get("ambient_strength", 0.3))
    return textwrap.dedent(f"""
        # Lighting
        bpy.ops.object.light_add(type='SUN', location=(0, 0, 5))
        _sun = bpy.context.active_object
        _sun.data.energy = {energy}
        _sun.rotation_euler = {rot}
        bpy.context.scene.world.node_tree.nodes['Background'].inputs[1].default_value = {ambient}
    """).strip()


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Main compile function
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def compile_scene_graph(scene_graph: dict, output_glb: str) -> str:
    """
    Compile a scene graph dict into a Blender Python script string.

    Args:
        scene_graph: The parsed scene graph JSON.
        output_glb:  Absolute path where the exported .glb should be written.

    Returns:
        A string containing the full Blender Python script.
    """
    objects: list[dict] = scene_graph.get("objects", [])
    camera: dict = scene_graph.get("camera", {})
    lighting: dict = scene_graph.get("lighting", {})

    sections: list[str] = []

    # --- Preamble ---
    sections.append(textwrap.dedent("""
        import bpy
        import math

        # Clear scene
        bpy.ops.object.select_all(action='SELECT')
        bpy.ops.object.delete(use_global=False)
        for c in bpy.data.collections:
            bpy.data.collections.remove(c)

        # Ensure world node tree exists
        if not bpy.context.scene.world:
            bpy.context.scene.world = bpy.data.worlds.new("World")
        bpy.context.scene.world.use_nodes = True
    """).strip())

    # --- Camera ---
    sections.append(_camera_block(camera or {}))

    # --- Lighting ---
    sections.append(_lighting_block(lighting or {}))

    # --- Objects ---
    # Metaball group registry: group_name → (data_var, obj_var)
    metaball_groups: dict[str, tuple[str, str]] = {}
    # Track which metaball groups have received a material assignment
    metaball_mat_assigned: set[str] = set()

    for obj in objects:
        obj_id: str = obj.get("id", "unknown")
        obj_type: str = obj.get("type", "sphere")
        mat: dict = obj.get("material", {})
        parent_id: str | None = obj.get("parent")

        part_lines: list[str] = [f"\n# --- {obj_id} ({obj_type}) ---"]

        # Create geometry
        if obj_type == "cylinder":
            part_lines.append(_cylinder_block(obj_id, obj))
        elif obj_type == "sphere":
            part_lines.append(_sphere_block(obj_id, obj))
        elif obj_type == "cube":
            part_lines.append(_cube_block(obj_id, obj))
        elif obj_type == "torus":
            part_lines.append(_torus_block(obj_id, obj))
        elif obj_type == "cone":
            part_lines.append(_cone_block(obj_id, obj))
        elif obj_type == "plane":
            part_lines.append(_plane_block(obj_id, obj))
        elif obj_type == "metaball":
            part_lines.append(_metaball_block(obj_id, obj, metaball_groups))
        elif obj_type == "imported_glb":
            part_lines.append(_imported_glb_block(obj_id, obj))
        else:
            part_lines.append(f"# Unknown type '{obj_type}' — skipping")
            sections.append("\n".join(part_lines))
            continue

        # Transform (rotation, scale)
        transform = _transform_block(obj_id, obj)
        if transform:
            part_lines.append(transform)

        # Parenting
        parent_code = _parent_block(obj_id, parent_id)
        if parent_code:
            part_lines.append(parent_code)

        # Material — for metaballs assign once per group
        if mat:
            if obj_type == "metaball":
                group = obj.get("metaball_group", obj_id)
                if group not in metaball_mat_assigned:
                    metaball_mat_assigned.add(group)
                    part_lines.append(_material_block(obj_id, mat))
                    # Assign to the group object
                    _, mball_obj_var = metaball_groups[group]
                    mvar = _mat_var(obj_id)
                    part_lines.append(
                        f"if {mball_obj_var}.data.materials:\n"
                        f"    {mball_obj_var}.data.materials[0] = {mvar}\n"
                        f"else:\n"
                        f"    {mball_obj_var}.data.materials.append({mvar})"
                    )
            else:
                part_lines.append(_material_block(obj_id, mat))
                part_lines.append(_material_assign_block(obj_id))

        sections.append("\n".join(part_lines))

    # --- Export ---
    sections.append(textwrap.dedent(f"""
        # Export scene as GLB
        bpy.ops.object.select_all(action='SELECT')
        bpy.ops.export_scene.gltf(
            filepath="{output_glb}",
            export_format='GLB',
            use_selection=False,
            export_apply=True,
        )
        print("EXPORT_DONE: {output_glb}")
    """).strip())

    return "\n\n".join(sections)


def write_script(scene_graph: dict, script_path: str, output_glb: str) -> str:
    """Write the compiled script to disk and return the script content."""
    script = compile_scene_graph(scene_graph, output_glb)
    Path(script_path).write_text(script, encoding="utf-8")
    return script

        ]

            f"_bsdf.inputs['Subsurface Weight'].default_value = {subsurface}",
            f"_bsdf.inputs['Subsurface Radius'].default_value = {ssc[:3]}",
        ]

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Object creation blocks per type
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _cylinder_block(obj_id: str, obj: dict) -> str:

    radius = float(params.get("radius", 0.5))
    depth = float(params.get("depth", 1.0))
    loc = _vec3(obj.get("position"))
    var = _obj_var(obj_id)
    return "\n".join([
        f"bpy.ops.mesh.primitive_cylinder_add(radius={radius}, depth={depth}, location={loc})",
        f"{var} = bpy.context.active_object",
        f'{var}.name = "{obj_id}"',
    ])


def _sphere_block(obj_id: str, obj: dict) -> str:
    params = obj.get("params", {})
    radius = float(params.get("radius", 0.5))
    loc = _vec3(obj.get("position"))
    var = _obj_var(obj_id)
    return "\n".join([
        f"bpy.ops.mesh.primitive_uv_sphere_add(radius={radius}, location={loc})",
        f"{var} = bpy.context.active_object",
        f'{var}.name = "{obj_id}"',
    ])


def _cube_block(obj_id: str, obj: dict) -> str:
    params = obj.get("params", {})
    size = params.get("size", [1, 1, 1])
    if isinstance(size, (int, float)):
        size = [size, size, size]
    loc = _vec3(obj.get("position"))
    var = _obj_var(obj_id)
    # Create unit cube then scale to desired size
    sx, sy, sz = float(size[0]), float(size[1]), float(size[2])
    return "\n".join([
        f"bpy.ops.mesh.primitive_cube_add(size=1, location={loc})",
        f"{var} = bpy.context.active_object",
        f'{var}.name = "{obj_id}"',
        f"{var}.scale = ({sx}, {sy}, {sz})",
        f"bpy.ops.object.transform_apply(scale=True)",
    ])


def _torus_block(obj_id: str, obj: dict) -> str:
    params = obj.get("params", {})
    major_r = float(params.get("major_radius", 0.5))
    minor_r = float(params.get("minor_radius", 0.1))
    loc = _vec3(obj.get("position"))
    var = _obj_var(obj_id)
    return "\n".join([
        f"bpy.ops.mesh.primitive_torus_add("
        f"major_radius={major_r}, minor_radius={minor_r}, location={loc})",
        f"{var} = bpy.context.active_object",
        f'{var}.name = "{obj_id}"',
    ])


def _cone_block(obj_id: str, obj: dict) -> str:
    params = obj.get("params", {})
    r1 = float(params.get("radius1", 0.5))
    r2 = float(params.get("radius2", 0.0))
    depth = float(params.get("depth", 1.0))
    loc = _vec3(obj.get("position"))
    var = _obj_var(obj_id)
    return "\n".join([
        f"bpy.ops.mesh.primitive_cone_add("
        f"radius1={r1}, radius2={r2}, depth={depth}, location={loc})",
        f"{var} = bpy.context.active_object",
        f'{var}.name = "{obj_id}"',
    ])


def _plane_block(obj_id: str, obj: dict) -> str:
    params = obj.get("params", {})
    size = float(params.get("size", 2.0))
    loc = _vec3(obj.get("position"))
    var = _obj_var(obj_id)
    return "\n".join([
        f"bpy.ops.mesh.primitive_plane_add(size={size}, location={loc})",
        f"{var} = bpy.context.active_object",
        f'{var}.name = "{obj_id}"',
    ])


def _metaball_block(obj_id: str, obj: dict, group_vars: dict[str, str]) -> str:
    """
    Metaballs in a shared group all blend seamlessly. We reuse one bpy.data.metaballs
    object per group and add elements to it.
    """
    params = obj.get("params", {})
    radius = float(params.get("radius", 0.3))
    stiffness = float(params.get("stiffness", 1.0))
    pos = _vec3(obj.get("position"))
    group = obj.get("metaball_group", obj_id)
    var = _obj_var(obj_id)

    if group not in group_vars:
        # First metaball in this group — create the metaball data object
        mball_data_var = f"mball_{group}"
        mball_obj_var = f"mball_obj_{group}"
        group_vars[group] = (mball_data_var, mball_obj_var)
        lines = [
            f'{mball_data_var} = bpy.data.metaballs.new("{group}")',
            f'{mball_data_var}.resolution = 0.05',
            f'{mball_data_var}.render_resolution = 0.02',
            f'{mball_obj_var} = bpy.data.objects.new("{group}", {mball_data_var})',
            f"bpy.context.collection.objects.link({mball_obj_var})",
        ]
    else:
        mball_data_var, mball_obj_var = group_vars[group]
        lines = []

    elem_var = f"elem_{obj_id}"
    lines += [
        f"{elem_var} = {mball_data_var}.elements.new()",
        f"{elem_var}.type = 'BALL'",
        f"{elem_var}.co = {pos}",
        f"{elem_var}.radius = {radius}",
        f"{elem_var}.stiffness = {stiffness}",
        f"{var} = {mball_obj_var}",   # alias so material assignment works uniformly
    ]
    return "\n".join(lines)


def _imported_glb_block(obj_id: str, obj: dict) -> str:
    params = obj.get("params", {})
    filepath = params.get("filepath", "")
    loc = _vec3(obj.get("position"))
    var = _obj_var(obj_id)
    return "\n".join([
        f'bpy.ops.import_scene.gltf(filepath="{filepath}")',
        f"_imported = bpy.context.selected_objects",
        f"{var} = _imported[0] if _imported else None",
        f"if {var}:",
        f'    {var}.name = "{obj_id}"',
        f"    {var}.location = {loc}",
    ])


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Rotation / scale / parent
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _transform_block(obj_id: str, obj: dict) -> str:
    var = _obj_var(obj_id)
    lines = []
    rot = obj.get("rotation_euler")
    if rot and any(v != 0 for v in rot):
        rx, ry, rz = float(rot[0]), float(rot[1]), float(rot[2])
        lines.append(f"{var}.rotation_euler = ({rx}, {ry}, {rz})")

    scale = obj.get("scale")
    if scale and scale != [1, 1, 1]:
        sx, sy, sz = float(scale[0]), float(scale[1]), float(scale[2])
        lines.append(f"{var}.scale = ({sx}, {sy}, {sz})")
    return "\n".join(lines)


def _parent_block(obj_id: str, parent_id: str | None) -> str:
    if not parent_id:
        return ""
    var = _obj_var(obj_id)
    pvar = _obj_var(parent_id)
    return "\n".join([
        f"if {pvar} and {var}:",
        f"    {var}.parent = {pvar}",
        f"    {var}.matrix_parent_inverse = {pvar}.matrix_world.inverted()",
    ])


def _material_assign_block(obj_id: str) -> str:
    var = _obj_var(obj_id)
    mvar = _mat_var(obj_id)
    return "\n".join([
        f"if {var} and {var}.data and hasattr({var}.data, 'materials'):",
        f"    if {var}.data.materials:",
        f"        {var}.data.materials[0] = {mvar}",
        f"    else:",
        f"        {var}.data.materials.append({mvar})",
    ])


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Camera and lighting
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _camera_block(camera: dict) -> str:
    loc = _vec3(camera.get("location", [3, -5, 3]))
    look_at = _vec3(camera.get("look_at", [0, 0, 0]))
    lens = float(camera.get("lens_mm", 50))
    return textwrap.dedent(f"""
        # Camera
        bpy.ops.object.camera_add(location={loc})
        _cam = bpy.context.active_object
        _cam.name = "SceneCamera"
        _cam.data.lens = {lens}
        import mathutils
        _direction = mathutils.Vector({look_at}) - mathutils.Vector({loc})
        _rot_quat = _direction.to_track_quat('-Z', 'Y')
        _cam.rotation_euler = _rot_quat.to_euler()
        bpy.context.scene.camera = _cam
    """).strip()


def _lighting_block(lighting: dict) -> str:
    energy = float(lighting.get("sun_energy", 3.0))
    rot = _vec3(lighting.get("sun_rotation_euler", [0.6, 0, 0.8]))
    ambient = float(lighting.get("ambient_strength", 0.3))
    return textwrap.dedent(f"""
        # Lighting
        bpy.ops.object.light_add(type='SUN', location=(0, 0, 5))
        _sun = bpy.context.active_object
        _sun.data.energy = {energy}
        _sun.rotation_euler = {rot}
        bpy.context.scene.world.node_tree.nodes['Background'].inputs[1].default_value = {ambient}
    """).strip()


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Main compile function
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def compile_scene_graph(scene_graph: dict, output_glb: str) -> str:
    """
    Compile a scene graph dict into a Blender Python script string.

    Args:
        scene_graph: The parsed scene graph JSON.
        output_glb:  Absolute path where the exported .glb should be written.

    Returns:
        A string containing the full Blender Python script.
    """
    objects: list[dict] = scene_graph.get("objects", [])
    camera: dict = scene_graph.get("camera", {})
    lighting: dict = scene_graph.get("lighting", {})

    sections: list[str] = []

    # --- Preamble ---
    sections.append(textwrap.dedent("""
        import bpy
        import math

        # Clear scene
        bpy.ops.object.select_all(action='SELECT')
        bpy.ops.object.delete(use_global=False)
        for c in bpy.data.collections:
            bpy.data.collections.remove(c)

        # Ensure world node tree exists
        if not bpy.context.scene.world:
            bpy.context.scene.world = bpy.data.worlds.new("World")
        bpy.context.scene.world.use_nodes = True
    """).strip())

    # --- Camera ---
    sections.append(_camera_block(camera or {}))

    # --- Lighting ---
    sections.append(_lighting_block(lighting or {}))

    # --- Objects ---
    # Metaball group registry: group_name → (data_var, obj_var)
    metaball_groups: dict[str, tuple[str, str]] = {}
    # Track which metaball groups have received a material assignment
    metaball_mat_assigned: set[str] = set()

    for obj in objects:
        obj_id: str = obj.get("id", "unknown")
        obj_type: str = obj.get("type", "sphere")
        mat: dict = obj.get("material", {})
        parent_id: str | None = obj.get("parent")

        part_lines: list[str] = [f"\n# --- {obj_id} ({obj_type}) ---"]

        # Create geometry
        if obj_type == "cylinder":
            part_lines.append(_cylinder_block(obj_id, obj))
        elif obj_type == "sphere":
            part_lines.append(_sphere_block(obj_id, obj))
        elif obj_type == "cube":
            part_lines.append(_cube_block(obj_id, obj))
        elif obj_type == "torus":
            part_lines.append(_torus_block(obj_id, obj))
        elif obj_type == "cone":
            part_lines.append(_cone_block(obj_id, obj))
        elif obj_type == "plane":
            part_lines.append(_plane_block(obj_id, obj))
        elif obj_type == "metaball":
            part_lines.append(_metaball_block(obj_id, obj, metaball_groups))
        elif obj_type == "imported_glb":
            part_lines.append(_imported_glb_block(obj_id, obj))
        else:
            part_lines.append(f"# Unknown type '{obj_type}' — skipping")
            sections.append("\n".join(part_lines))
            continue

        # Transform (rotation, scale)
        transform = _transform_block(obj_id, obj)
        if transform:
            part_lines.append(transform)

        # Parenting
        parent_code = _parent_block(obj_id, parent_id)
        if parent_code:
            part_lines.append(parent_code)

        # Material — for metaballs assign once per group
        if mat:
            if obj_type == "metaball":
                group = obj.get("metaball_group", obj_id)
                if group not in metaball_mat_assigned:
                    metaball_mat_assigned.add(group)
                    part_lines.append(_material_block(obj_id, mat))
                    # Assign to the group object
                    _, mball_obj_var = metaball_groups[group]
                    mvar = _mat_var(obj_id)
                    part_lines.append(
                        f"if {mball_obj_var}.data.materials:\n"
                        f"    {mball_obj_var}.data.materials[0] = {mvar}\n"
                        f"else:\n"
                        f"    {mball_obj_var}.data.materials.append({mvar})"
                    )
            else:
                part_lines.append(_material_block(obj_id, mat))
                part_lines.append(_material_assign_block(obj_id))

        sections.append("\n".join(part_lines))

    # --- Export ---
    sections.append(textwrap.dedent(f"""
        # Export scene as GLB
        bpy.ops.object.select_all(action='SELECT')
        bpy.ops.export_scene.gltf(
            filepath="{output_glb}",
            export_format='GLB',
            use_selection=False,
            export_apply=True,
        )
        print("EXPORT_DONE: {output_glb}")
    """).strip())

    return "\n\n".join(sections)


def write_script(scene_graph: dict, script_path: str, output_glb: str) -> str:
    """Write the compiled script to disk and return the script content."""
    script = compile_scene_graph(scene_graph, output_glb)
    Path(script_path).write_text(script, encoding="utf-8")
    return script

input.content:
"""
agent.py — Claude-powered scene graph generation agent.

Supports:
  - Text-to-scene: generate a full scene graph from a description.
  - Image-to-scene: analyse an uploaded image then generate the scene.
  - Multi-turn refinement: apply incremental changes on top of an existing scene graph.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import base64
import json
import os
import re
import uuid
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any

import anthropic

from part_library import SCENE_GRAPH_SCHEMA, get_template_context

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# System prompt
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

SYSTEM_PROMPT = f"""You are BlenderAgent, an expert 3D scene designer that outputs structured scene graph JSON.

## Your job
Given a user description (text and/or image), produce a complete, valid scene graph JSON that will be
compiled into a Blender Python script. The scene must be built from atomic geometric parts assembled
together — this is called emergent, part-based composition.

## Core rules
1. ALWAYS output valid JSON inside a ```json … ``` code fence. No other text outside the fence.
2. Every object needs a unique snake_case `id`.
3. Use real-world proportions (metres). A human is ~1.7 m tall, a bicycle ~1.8 m long.
4. For organic shapes (humans, animals, creatures) use `"type": "metaball"` parts all sharing the
   same `"metaball_group"` string. Blender merges them seamlessly.
5. For female human figures use these proportions:
   - Hip width > shoulder width (ratio ~1.15)
   - Narrower waist (abdomen radius ≈ 0.68 × pelvis radius)
   - Fuller thighs (thigh radius ≈ ribcage radius)
   - Softer curves overall (lower stiffness 0.8–1.0)
6. Camera should frame the scene nicely. Default: location [3, -4, 2.5], look_at scene centre.
7. Provide plausible materials with `base_color` [R,G,B,A], `roughness`, `metallic`.
8. Positions use Z-up (Blender convention). Ground plane is Z=0.
9. When refining, return the COMPLETE updated scene graph, not just the changed parts.

## Schema
{SCENE_GRAPH_SCHEMA}

{get_template_context()}

## Output format (strictly)
```json
{{ … full scene graph … }}
```
"""


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Session store (in-memory; swap for Redis/DB in production)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

_sessions: dict[str, dict] = {}


def _new_session() -> str:
    sid = str(uuid.uuid4())
    _sessions[sid] = {
        "history": [],          # list of {"role": ..., "content": ...}
        "scene_graph": None,    # latest parsed scene graph
    }
    return sid


def get_session(session_id: str | None) -> tuple[str, dict]:
    """Return (session_id, session_data), creating a new session if needed."""
    if session_id and session_id in _sessions:
        return session_id, _sessions[session_id]
    sid = _new_session()
    return sid, _sessions[sid]


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Image helpers
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _encode_image(image_path: str) -> dict:
    """Return an Anthropic image content block from a local file path."""
    data = Path(image_path).read_bytes()
    b64 = base64.standard_b64encode(data).decode("utf-8")
    suffix = Path(image_path).suffix.lower()
    media_map = {".jpg": "image/jpeg", ".jpeg": "image/jpeg",
                 ".png": "image/png", ".webp": "image/webp", ".gif": "image/gif"}
    media_type = media_map.get(suffix, "image/png")
    return {
        "type": "image",
        "source": {"type": "base64", "media_type": media_type, "data": b64},
    }


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# JSON extraction
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

_JSON_RE = re.compile(r"```json\s*([\s\S]+?)\s*```", re.IGNORECASE)


def _extract_json(text: str) -> dict:
    m = _JSON_RE.search(text)
    if m:
        raw = m.group(1)
    else:
        # Fallback: try raw text
        raw = text.strip()
    try:
        return json.loads(raw)
    except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:
        raise ValueError(f"Claude returned invalid JSON: {exc}\n\nRaw:\n{raw[:500]}") from exc


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Main agent call
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def generate_scene(
    prompt: str,
    session_id: str | None = None,
    image_paths: list[str] | None = None,
    glb_paths: list[str] | None = None,
) -> dict:
    """
    Generate or refine a scene graph.

    Args:
        prompt:      Natural language instruction.
        session_id:  Existing session for multi-turn refinement; None to start fresh.
        image_paths: Local paths to uploaded images (for vision analysis).
        glb_paths:   Local paths to uploaded GLB assets (referenced in the scene graph).

    Returns:
        {
            "session_id": str,
            "scene_graph": dict,
            "raw_response": str,
        }
    """
    client = anthropic.Anthropic(api_key=os.environ["ANTHROPIC_API_KEY"])
    sid, session = get_session(session_id)

    # Build the user content block
    content: list[dict] = []

    # Attach images for vision
    if image_paths:
        for img_path in image_paths:
            if Path(img_path).exists():
                content.append(_encode_image(img_path))
                content.append({
                    "type": "text",
                    "text": f"[Uploaded image: {Path(img_path).name}]",
                })

    # Mention GLB assets so Claude can reference their paths in the scene graph
    if glb_paths:
        glb_note = "Uploaded GLB assets (use these filepaths in imported_glb objects):\n"
        glb_note += "\n".join(f"  - {p}" for p in glb_paths)
        content.append({"type": "text", "text": glb_note})

    # Include current scene graph for refinement
    if session["scene_graph"]:
        current_json = json.dumps(session["scene_graph"], indent=2)
        content.append({
            "type": "text",
            "text": (
                f"Current scene graph (modify this according to the instruction):\n"
                f"```json\n{current_json}\n```\n\n"
                f"Instruction: {prompt}"
            ),
        })
    else:
        content.append({"type": "text", "text": prompt})

    # Append to conversation history
    session["history"].append({"role": "user", "content": content})

    # Call Claude
    response = client.messages.create(
        model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
        max_tokens=8192,
        system=SYSTEM_PROMPT,
        messages=session["history"],
    )

    assistant_text: str = response.content[0].text
    session["history"].append({"role": "assistant", "content": assistant_text})

    # Parse scene graph
    scene_graph = _extract_json(assistant_text)
    # Stamp a scene_id if missing
    if "scene_id" not in scene_graph:
        scene_graph["scene_id"] = str(uuid.uuid4())
    session["scene_graph"] = scene_graph

    return {
        "session_id": sid,
        "scene_graph": scene_graph,
        "raw_response": assistant_text,
    }

def generate_scene(
    prompt: str,

    Args:
        prompt:      Natural language instruction.
        session_id:  Existing session for multi-turn refinement; None to start fresh.
        image_paths: Local paths to uploaded images (for vision analysis).
        glb_paths:   Local paths to uploaded GLB assets (referenced in the scene graph).

    # Attach images for vision
    if image_paths:
        for img_path in image_paths:
            if Path(img_path).exists():
                content.append(_encode_image(img_path))
                content.append({
                    "type": "text",
                    "text": f"[Uploaded image: {Path(img_path).name}]",
                })

    # Mention GLB assets so Claude can reference their paths in the scene graph
    if glb_paths:
        glb_note = "Uploaded GLB assets (use these filepaths in imported_glb objects):\n"
        glb_note += "\n".join(f"  - {p}" for p in glb_paths)
        content.append({"type": "text", "text": glb_note})

    # Include current scene graph for refinement
    if session["scene_graph"]:
        current_json = json.dumps(session["scene_graph"], indent=2)
        content.append({
            "type": "text",
            "text": (
                f"Current scene graph (modify this according to the instruction):\n"
                f"```json\n{current_json}\n```\n\n"
                f"Instruction: {prompt}"
            ),
        })
    else:
        content.append({"type": "text", "text": prompt})

    # Append to conversation history
    session["history"].append({"role": "user", "content": content})

    # Call Claude
    response = client.messages.create(
        model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
        max_tokens=8192,
        system=SYSTEM_PROMPT,
        messages=session["history"],
    )

    assistant_text: str = response.content[0].text
    session["history"].append({"role": "assistant", "content": assistant_text})

    # Parse scene graph
    scene_graph = _extract_json(assistant_text)
    # Stamp a scene_id if missing
    if "scene_id" not in scene_graph:
        scene_graph["scene_id"] = str(uuid.uuid4())
    session["scene_graph"] = scene_graph

    return {
        "session_id": sid,
        "scene_graph": scene_graph,
        "raw_response": assistant_text,
    }

"""
main.py — FastAPI backend for the Blender AI Agent.

Endpoints:
  POST /api/generate          — text prompt → scene graph + Blender script
  POST /api/refine            — multi-turn refinement of an existing scene
  POST /api/render            — compile scene graph and run Blender headlessly → GLB
  POST /api/upload-image      — upload an image for vision analysis
  POST /api/upload-glb        — upload a GLB asset to embed in scenes
  GET  /api/scene/{scene_id}  — fetch an existing scene graph
  GET  /api/download/{filename} — download a generated GLB
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import asyncio
import os
import shutil
import subprocess
import tempfile
import uuid
from pathlib import Path

import aiofiles
from dotenv import load_dotenv
from fastapi import FastAPI, File, Form, HTTPException, UploadFile
from fastapi.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware
from fastapi.responses import FileResponse, JSONResponse
from pydantic import BaseModel

import agent as agent_module
import compiler

load_dotenv()

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# App setup
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

app = FastAPI(title="Blender AI Agent", version="1.0.0")

app.add_middleware(
    CORSMiddleware,
    allow_origins=["*"],
    allow_credentials=True,
    allow_methods=["*"],
    allow_headers=["*"],
)

BASE_DIR = Path(__file__).parent
UPLOADS_DIR = BASE_DIR / "uploads"
OUTPUTS_DIR = BASE_DIR / "outputs"
UPLOADS_DIR.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
OUTPUTS_DIR.mkdir(exist_ok=True)

BLENDER_BIN = shutil.which("blender") or "/snap/bin/blender"

# In-memory scene graph store (scene_id → scene_graph dict)
_scene_store: dict[str, dict] = {}


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Request / response models
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class GenerateRequest(BaseModel):
    prompt: str
    session_id: str | None = None


class RefineRequest(BaseModel):
    prompt: str
    session_id: str


class RenderRequest(BaseModel):
    scene_id: str


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helpers
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

async def _save_upload(upload: UploadFile, dest_dir: Path) -> str:
    ext = Path(upload.filename).suffix if upload.filename else ""
    filename = f"{uuid.uuid4()}{ext}"
    dest = dest_dir / filename
    async with aiofiles.open(dest, "wb") as f:
        content = await upload.read()
        await f.write(content)
    return str(dest)


async def _run_blender(script_path: str) -> tuple[int, str, str]:
    """Run Blender headlessly and return (returncode, stdout, stderr)."""
    proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(
        BLENDER_BIN,
        "--background",
        "--python", script_path,
        stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,
        stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,
    )
    stdout, stderr = await asyncio.wait_for(proc.communicate(), timeout=120)
    return proc.returncode, stdout.decode(errors="replace"), stderr.decode(errors="replace")


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Routes
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@app.get("/api/health")
async def health():
    return {"status": "ok", "blender": BLENDER_BIN}


@app.post("/api/generate")
async def generate(req: GenerateRequest):
    """Generate a scene graph from a text prompt (no images)."""
    if not req.prompt.strip():
        raise HTTPException(400, "prompt is required")

    result = agent_module.generate_scene(
        prompt=req.prompt,
        session_id=req.session_id,
    )
    scene_graph = result["scene_graph"]
    _scene_store[scene_graph["scene_id"]] = scene_graph

    return {
        "session_id": result["session_id"],
        "scene_id": scene_graph["scene_id"],
        "scene_graph": scene_graph,
    }


@app.post("/api/generate-with-media")
async def generate_with_media(
    prompt: str = Form(...),
    session_id: str | None = Form(None),
    images: list[UploadFile] = File(default=[]),
    glbs: list[UploadFile] = File(default=[]),
):
    """Generate a scene from a prompt plus optional image/GLB uploads."""
    image_paths: list[str] = []
    glb_paths: list[str] = []

    for img in images:
        if img.filename:
            path = await _save_upload(img, UPLOADS_DIR)
            image_paths.append(path)

    for glb in glbs:
        if glb.filename:
            path = await _save_upload(glb, UPLOADS_DIR)
            glb_paths.append(path)

    result = agent_module.generate_scene(
        prompt=prompt,
        session_id=session_id,
        image_paths=image_paths or None,
        glb_paths=glb_paths or None,
    )
    scene_graph = result["scene_graph"]
    _scene_store[scene_graph["scene_id"]] = scene_graph

    return {
        "session_id": result["session_id"],
        "scene_id": scene_graph["scene_id"],
        "scene_graph": scene_graph,
        "uploaded_images": image_paths,
        "uploaded_glbs": glb_paths,
    }


@app.post("/api/refine")
async def refine(req: RefineRequest):
    """Apply a natural-language refinement to the current session's scene."""
    result = agent_module.generate_scene(
        prompt=req.prompt,
        session_id=req.session_id,
    )
    scene_graph = result["scene_graph"]
    _scene_store[scene_graph["scene_id"]] = scene_graph

    return {
        "session_id": result["session_id"],
        "scene_id": scene_graph["scene_id"],
        "scene_graph": scene_graph,
    }


@app.post("/api/render")
async def render(req: RenderRequest):
    """
    Compile the stored scene graph into a Blender script and run it headlessly.
    Returns the path to the generated GLB file.
    """
    scene_graph = _scene_store.get(req.scene_id)
    if not scene_graph:
        raise HTTPException(404, f"scene_id '{req.scene_id}' not found")

    scene_id = req.scene_id
    script_path = str(OUTPUTS_DIR / f"{scene_id}.py")
    glb_path = str(OUTPUTS_DIR / f"{scene_id}.glb")

    compiler.write_script(scene_graph, script_path, glb_path)

    returncode, stdout, stderr = await _run_blender(script_path)

    if returncode != 0 or not Path(glb_path).exists():
        # Return the Blender error for debugging
        raise HTTPException(500, {
            "error": "Blender render failed",
            "returncode": returncode,
            "stdout": stdout[-3000:],
            "stderr": stderr[-3000:],
        })

    return {
        "scene_id": scene_id,
        "glb_url": f"/api/download/{scene_id}.glb",
        "script_url": f"/api/download/{scene_id}.py",
        "stdout": stdout[-2000:],
    }


@app.get("/api/scene/{scene_id}")
async def get_scene(scene_id: str):
    scene_graph = _scene_store.get(scene_id)
    if not scene_graph:
        raise HTTPException(404, "Scene not found")
    return scene_graph


@app.get("/api/download/{filename}")
async def download(filename: str):
    # Sanitise filename — no path traversal
    safe = Path(filename).name
    path = OUTPUTS_DIR / safe
    if not path.exists():
        raise HTTPException(404, "File not found")
    media = "model/gltf-binary" if safe.endswith(".glb") else "text/plain"
    return FileResponse(str(path), media_type=media, filename=safe)


@app.post("/api/upload-image")
async def upload_image(file: UploadFile = File(...)):
    path = await _save_upload(file, UPLOADS_DIR)
    return {"path": path, "filename": Path(path).name}


@app.post("/api/upload-glb")
async def upload_glb(file: UploadFile = File(...)):
    path = await _save_upload(file, UPLOADS_DIR)
    return {"path": path, "filename": Path(path).name}

input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Start both backend and frontend

set -euo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
BACKEND="$ROOT/backend"
FRONTEND="$ROOT/frontend"

G='\033[0;32m'; C='\033[0;36m'; Y='\033[1;33m'; X='\033[0m'; B='\033[1m'

log() { echo -e "${C}[$(date +%H:%M:%S)]${X} $*"; }
ok()  { echo -e "${G}✔${X}  $*"; }
err() { echo -e "${Y}✘${X}  $*"; }

cleanup() {
  echo ""
  log "Stopping services…"
  kill "$BACKEND_PID" "$FRONTEND_PID" 2>/dev/null || true
}
trap cleanup EXIT INT TERM

# ── Backend ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
log "Setting up Python backend…"
if [[ ! -d "$BACKEND/venv" ]]; then
  python3 -m venv "$BACKEND/venv"
  log "Installing backend dependencies…"
  "$BACKEND/venv/bin/pip" install -q -r "$BACKEND/requirements.txt"
fi

if [[ ! -f "$BACKEND/.env" ]]; then
  if [[ -z "${ANTHROPIC_API_KEY:-}" ]]; then
    err "No ANTHROPIC_API_KEY found. Copy backend/.env.example → backend/.env and add your key."
    exit 1
  fi
  echo "ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=$ANTHROPIC_API_KEY" > "$BACKEND/.env"
fi

log "Starting FastAPI backend on :8000…"
cd "$BACKEND"
"$BACKEND/venv/bin/uvicorn" main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000 --reload &
BACKEND_PID=$!

# ── Frontend ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
log "Setting up React frontend…"
if [[ ! -d "$FRONTEND/node_modules" ]]; then
  log "Installing frontend dependencies…"
  cd "$FRONTEND" && npm install --silent
fi

log "Starting Vite frontend on :5173…"
cd "$FRONTEND"
npm run dev &
FRONTEND_PID=$!

# ── Dashboard ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
sleep 2
echo ""
echo -e "${B}${G}╔══════════════════════════════════════════════╗${X}"
echo -e "${B}${G}║      Blender AI Agent — Running              ║${X}"
echo -e "${B}${G}╚══════════════════════════════════════════════╝${X}"
echo ""
echo -e "  ${G}●${X} ${B}Frontend${X}   http://localhost:5173"
echo -e "  ${G}●${X} ${B}Backend${X}    http://localhost:8000"
echo -e "  ${G}●${X} ${B}API docs${X}   http://localhost:8000/docs"
echo ""
echo -e "  ${Y}Blender:${X} $(blender --version 2>/dev/null | head -1 || echo 'not found')"
echo ""
echo -e "  ${C}Press Ctrl+C to stop${X}"
echo ""

wait

content.text:
Everything is green. Here's a full summary of what was built:

---

## Blender AI Agent — Complete

**Location:** `/home/nugad/blender-ai-agent/`

### To start
```bash
cd /home/nugad/blender-ai-agent

# Add your API key
echo "ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-..." > backend/.env

bash start.sh
# Frontend → http://localhost:5173
# Backend  → http://localhost:8000
# API docs → http://localhost:8000/docs
```

---

### Architecture

```
frontend (React + Three.js)          backend (FastAPI)
  ChatPanel   → text/refine   →  POST /api/generate
  DropZone    → images/GLBs   →  POST /api/generate-with-media
  [Render]    → trigger       →  POST /api/render
  SceneGraph  → inspect JSON         ↓
  ScenePreview← GLB download  ←  GET  /api/download/{id}
                                     ↓
                               agent.py  (Claude claude-sonnet-4-6 + vision)
                                     ↓
                               compiler.py  (scene graph → bpy script)
                                     ↓
                               blender --background --python script.py
                                     ↓
                               output/{scene_id}.glb
```

### Key features implemented
| Feature | How |
|---|---|
| **Emergent part composition** | Claude generates per-part JSON; compiler assembles each atomic piece separately in Blender |
| **Organic forms (female body)** | 26 metaballs in one shared group — Blender blends seamlessly; female proportions encoded in `part_library.py` |
| **Image understanding** | Vision API via base64 image blocks in Claude messages |
| **GLB drag-and-drop** | `react-dropzone` → `POST /api/generate-with-media`; paths injected into scene graph as `imported_glb` objects |
| **Multi-turn refinement** | `session["history"]` persists conversation; current scene graph prepended to each refinement turn |
| **Scene graph JSON** | Structured schema: id, type, params, position, rotation, material, parent, metaball_group |
| **Headless Blender** | `asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(blender --background --python ...)` with 120 s timeout |
| **Metaball → mesh fix** | Auto-converts metaball groups to mesh before GLB export (GLB exporter ignores raw metaball data) |
